mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants price in sustained borrowing costs.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained employment growth measured by PAYEMS increasing month-over-month drives positive earnings expectations and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth trajectories upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,300 thousand units signals weakening demand and forward economic contraction. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability and corporate earnings forecasts downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or post-shock demand destruction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST < 1,300, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. Combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL), this stagflationary signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors flee cyclical exposure.
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with supply constraints and persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when both INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL > 304.0, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerating above 2% month-over-month signals robust consumer demand and forward earnings growth. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices corporate profit margins and revenue growth upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 678,424 million dollars as of 2023-07-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of instances when RSAFS month-over-month growth > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 3.8% signals labor market deterioration and forward earnings pressure. When combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS), this dual signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as recession risk reprices upward.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase with rising unemployment and job losses
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE > 3.8% AND PAYEMS declines, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period, this signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates on future cash flows. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations expand.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle transitioning from restrictive to accommodative stance
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized rate combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS) signals demand-side weakness and forward earnings contraction. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,580.499 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2023-04-01
fact:RSAFS at 678,424 million dollars as of 2023-07-01
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early-stage recession with synchronized demand deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS declines, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
